Biology.subject: Pre-Medical Biology
Biology.title: MCAT Biology Syllabus (45% Weightage - 81 MCQs)
objectives: Classify viruses and explain HIV infection.
Outline cellular respiration of macromolecules.
Describe structure and function of biological molecules, DNA, and RNA.
Compare prokaryotic/eukaryotic cells and organelle functions.
Explain neuron structure, reflex arc, and brain function.
Describe enzyme action, factors, and inhibition.
Compare Lamarckism and Darwinism.
Describe human reproductive systems, menstrual cycle, and STDs.
Explain skeletal muscle ultrastructure and contraction.
Apply Mendel’s laws, gene linkage, and sex-linked inheritance.
Describe cardiac cycle, blood vessels, and lymphatic system.
Explain specific defense mechanisms in immunity.
Describe gas exchange and effects of smoking.
Describe digestive system structure and function.
Explain kidney function, thermoregulation, and excretion.
Describe biotech applications in vaccines, diagnosis, and treatment.

Chemistry.subject: Pre-Medical Chemistry
Chemistry.title: MCAT Chemistry Syllabus (25% Weightage - 45 MCQs)
objectives: Perform stoichiometric calculations (moles, limiting reactants, yield).
Describe quantum numbers, orbitals, and electronic configuration.
Apply gas laws (Boyle, Charles, Ideal Gas Equation).
Explain hydrogen bonding and anomalous behavior of water.
Differentiate crystalline, ionic, and molecular crystals.
Apply Le Chatelier's principle, solubility product, and buffer solutions.
Define rate of reaction, order of reaction, and activation energy.
Apply Hess’s law and first law of thermodynamics.
Balance redox reactions and define standard electrode potential.
Use VSEPR theory, hybridization, and dipole moment.
Describe periodic trends and reactions of Group I, II, IV elements.
Describe electronic structure of d-block elements.
Classify organic compounds, functional groups, and isomerism.
Describe free radical mechanism, alkenes, alkynes, and benzene reactions.
Describe nucleophilic substitution and elimination reactions.
Describe preparation and reactions of alcohols, phenols.
Describe nucleophilic addition in aldehydes and ketones.
Describe reactions and derivatives of carboxylic acids.
Classify proteins and describe enzymes as biocatalysts.
Describe types and uses of adhesives, dyes, and polymers.

Physics.subject: Pre-Medical Physics
Physics.title: MCAT Physics Syllabus (20% Weightage - 36 MCQs)
objectives: Determine sum and products of vectors.
Analyze projectile motion, Newton's laws, and collisions.
Explain work-energy theorem, power, and efficiency.
Relate angular and linear quantities.
Describe terminal velocity, equation of continuity, and Bernoulli’s equation.
Solve wave equation, superposition, stationary waves, and SHM.
Apply first law of thermodynamics and derive Cp - Cv = R.
Apply Coulomb’s law, electric field, potential, and capacitor charging/discharging.
State Ohm’s law, resistivity, internal resistance, and max power transfer.
Describe magnetic flux and motion of charged particle in magnetic field.
Apply Faraday’s law, Lenz’s law, and transformer principles.
Describe phase in AC circuits with resistor, capacitor, inductor.
Describe rectification and PN junction biasing.
Explain photon model of light.
Describe line spectra.
Describe half-life and medical uses of radiation.

English.subject: English Language
English.title: MCAT English Syllabus (5% Weightage - 9 MCQs)
objectives: Scan texts to answer short questions.
Deduce meanings from context and figurative language.
Use synonyms, irony, parody, and satire appropriately.
Illustrate pronoun-antecedent agreement, tenses, infinitives, gerunds.
Recognize varying positions of adverbs.
Illustrate use of prepositions and transitional devices.
Use punctuation marks correctly.
Analyze sentence classes and phrases.
Use active/passive voice and direct/indirect speech appropriately.
Proofread for errors in sentence structure, subject-verb agreement, spelling, and function.

Logical Reasoning.subject: Logical Reasoning
Logical Reasoning.title: MCAT Logical Reasoning Syllabus (5% Weightage - 9 MCQs)
objectives: Develop logical arguments to separate truth from falsehood.
Identify and critically evaluate false beliefs.
Develop arithmetical operations and geometrical progressions for series.
Develop sequential orders for letters and symbols.
Predict new relations based on given relations.
Develop new structures based on information from drawn structures.
Infer results from one problem to resolve another.
Develop skills to solve puzzles using deductive reasoning.
Gather information to make decisions and judge courses of action.
Give reasons for events and reject false beliefs through valid arguments.
